subtopics: Convert recurring decimals into fractions
Convert fractions to recurring decimals
topics.description: Convert recurring decimals into equivalent fractions using algebraic methods.

subtopics: Understand the meaning of surds
Simplify surd expressions
Manipulate surds including rationalising a denominator
Add, subtract and multiply surd expressions
topics.description: Understand and manipulate surds, including simplifying expressions and rationalising denominators.

subtopics: Use index laws to simplify numerical expressions
Evaluate expressions involving integer, fractional and negative powers
Express integers as a product of powers of prime factors
Find highest common factors (HCF) and lowest common multiples (LCM)
topics.description: Use index laws to simplify and evaluate numerical expressions involving integer, fractional and negative powers.

subtopics: Understand sets defined in algebraic terms and use subsets
Use Venn diagrams to represent sets and the number of elements in sets
Use the notation n(A) for the number of elements in set A
Use sets in practical situations including finding probabilities from Venn diagrams
topics.description: Understand and use set notation, Venn diagrams, and sets defined in algebraic terms to solve practical problems.

subtopics: Use repeated percentage change
Solve compound interest problems
Calculate total percentage increase/decrease after successive changes
Solve reverse percentage problems
topics.description: Use repeated percentage change and solve compound interest and depreciation problems.

subtopics: Identify upper and lower bounds where values are given to a degree of accuracy
Solve problems using upper and lower bounds
Calculate bounds for combinations of quantities
topics.description: Solve problems using upper and lower bounds where values are given to a degree of accuracy.

subtopics: Calculate with and interpret numbers in standard form
Solve problems involving standard form
topics.description: Calculate with and interpret numbers in the form a x 10^n where n is an integer and 1 ≤ a < 10.

subtopics: Expand the product of two or more linear expressions
Factorise quadratic expressions including ax² + bx + c
Manipulate algebraic fractions with numeric, linear or quadratic numerator/denominator
Complete the square for a given quadratic expression
Use algebra to support and construct proofs
topics.description: Expand products of linear expressions, factorise quadratics, manipulate algebraic fractions, complete the square, and construct proofs.

subtopics: Manipulate formulae to change the subject
Change subject where the subject appears twice
Change subject where a power of the subject occurs
topics.description: Manipulate formulae or equations to change the subject, including cases where the subject may appear twice or a power of the subject occurs.

subtopics: Set up problems involving direct or inverse proportion
Relate algebraic solutions to graphical representation of proportion equations
Work with y ∝ x, y ∝ 1/x, y ∝ x², y ∝ 1/x², y ∝ x³, y ∝ 1/x³, y ∝ √x, y ∝ 1/√x
topics.description: Set up problems involving direct or inverse proportion and relate algebraic solutions to graphical representations.

subtopics: Calculate the exact solution of two simultaneous linear equations in two unknowns
Interpret the equations as lines and the common solution as the point of intersection
Solve simultaneous equations where one is linear and the other is quadratic
topics.description: Solve simultaneous equations in two unknowns, interpreting linear equations as lines and their solution as the point of intersection, including one linear and one quadratic equation.

subtopics: Solve quadratic equations by factorisation
Solve quadratic equations using the quadratic formula
Solve quadratic equations by completing the square
Form and solve quadratic equations from data given in a context
Understand and use the discriminant
topics.description: Solve quadratic equations by factorisation, formula, and completing the square, and form equations from given contexts.

subtopics: Solve quadratic inequalities in one unknown
Represent the solution set of inequalities on a number line
Identify regions defined by linear inequalities
Shade regions defined by multiple linear inequalities
topics.description: Solve quadratic inequalities in one unknown and represent the solution set on a number line, and identify regions defined by linear inequalities.

subtopics: Understand and use common difference (d) and first term (a) in an arithmetic sequence
Know and use the nth term formula a + (n-1)d
Find the sum of the first n terms of an arithmetic series (Sn)
topics.description: Understand and use arithmetic sequences and series, including finding the nth term and the sum of the first n terms.

subtopics: Understand the concept that a function is a mapping between elements of two sets
Use function notations f(x) and f: x ↦ ...
Understand the terms domain and range
Identify values to exclude from a domain
Understand and find the composite function fg
Understand and find the inverse function f⁻¹
topics.description: Understand functions as mappings, use function notation, work with domain and range, and find composite and inverse functions.

subtopics: Recognise, plot and draw cubic graphs
Recognise, plot and draw reciprocal graphs
Recognise, plot and draw trigonometric graphs (sin, cos, tan) for angles of any size
Apply transformations to graphs: y = f(x) + a, y = f(ax), y = f(x + a), y = af(x)
Find the gradients of non-linear graphs by drawing a tangent
Find intersection points of linear and non-linear graphs
Find the equation of a straight line given two points
Find equations of lines parallel or perpendicular to a given line
topics.description: Recognise, plot and draw a range of graphs including cubic, reciprocal, and trigonometric functions, apply transformations, and work with straight line equations.

subtopics: Understand the concept of a variable rate of change
Differentiate integer powers of x
Determine gradients, rates of change, stationary points, turning points (maxima and minima) by differentiation
Distinguish between maxima and minima by considering the general shape of the graph
Apply calculus to linear kinematics and other simple practical problems
topics.description: Differentiate integer powers of x, determine gradients, stationary points and turning points, and apply calculus to kinematics and practical problems.

subtopics: Understand and use internal and external intersecting chord properties
Recognise the term cyclic quadrilateral
Angle subtended by an arc at the centre is twice the angle at the circumference
Angle subtended at the circumference by a diameter is a right angle
Angles in the same segment are equal
Sum of opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is 180 degrees
The alternate segment theorem
Provide geometrical reasons to support numerical values for angles
topics.description: Understand and use circle theorems including angle properties, cyclic quadrilaterals, intersecting chords, and the alternate segment theorem.

subtopics: Understand and use sine, cosine and tangent of obtuse angles
Understand and use angles of elevation and depression
Understand and use the sine and cosine rules for any triangle
Use Pythagoras theorem in three dimensions
Understand and use the formula ½ab sin C for the area of a triangle
Apply trigonometrical methods to solve problems in three dimensions including finding the angle between a line and a plane
topics.description: Use trigonometric functions for obtuse angles, apply sine and cosine rules, Pythagoras in 3D, and solve three-dimensional trigonometry problems.

subtopics: Find perimeters and areas of sectors of circles
Find the surface area and volume of a sphere
Find the surface area and volume of a right circular cone
Understand that areas of similar figures are in the ratio of the square of corresponding sides
Understand that volumes of similar figures are in the ratio of the cube of corresponding sides
Use areas and volumes of similar figures in solving problems
topics.description: Find perimeters and areas of sectors, surface areas and volumes of spheres and cones, and solve problems involving similar figures.

subtopics: Understand that a vector has both magnitude and direction
Use vector notation including column vectors
Multiply vectors by scalar quantities
Add and subtract vectors
Calculate the modulus (magnitude) of a vector
Find the resultant of two or more vectors
Apply vector methods for simple geometrical proofs
topics.description: Understand and use vectors including notation, scalar multiplication, addition and subtraction, modulus, resultants, and simple geometrical proofs.

subtopics: Construct and interpret histograms for continuous variables with unequal class intervals
Construct cumulative frequency diagrams from tabulated data
Use cumulative frequency diagrams
topics.description: Construct and interpret histograms and cumulative frequency diagrams for grouped data.

subtopics: Estimate the median from a cumulative frequency diagram
Understand the concept of a measure of spread
Find the interquartile range from a discrete data set
Estimate the interquartile range from a cumulative frequency diagram
topics.description: Estimate median and interquartile range from cumulative frequency diagrams and understand measures of spread.

subtopics: Draw and use tree diagrams
Determine the probability that two or more independent events will occur
Use simple conditional probability when combining events
Apply probability to simple problems
topics.description: Draw and use tree diagrams, calculate probabilities of independent events, and use simple conditional probability.
